sections.question_synthetic: Can candidates who were unopposed in the June election or won outright in non-partisan races participate in ballot issue campaigns in the November election without violating Regulation 18538?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The sender (Sheriff Craig) is requesting written confirmation of a prior verbal conversation with FPPC staff member John Rothman regarding Regulation 18538 interpretation. No formal question or conclusion sections present in this letter format. Document appears to be the citizen's correspondence to FPPC, not FPPC's advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can candidates who were unopposed in the June election or won outright in non-partisan races participate in ballot issue campaigns in the November election without violating Regulation 18538?

embedding.summary: Sacramento County Sheriff Glen Craig requests written confirmation from the FPPC that Regulation 18538 does not restrict candidates who are unopposed or won in June elections from participating in ballot issue campaigns in November, citing his involvement in Charter Amendment and state-wide proposition campaigns.
